htmlContent:
Document Information and Committee Acronyms

DRAFT

2021-2023 Geographic Information Coordination Council Goals and Strategic Direction

Committee Acronyms

  • GICC – Geographic Information Coordinating Council
  • LGC- Local Government Committee
  • SGUC – State Government GIS Users Committee
  • FIC- Federal Interagency Committee
  • TAC – Technical Advisory Committee
  • SMAC – Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee

Goal 1

Goal 1. Improve, expand, and support statewide geospatial data and applications.

Goal 1 Sub-goals 1.1 to 1.3 with associated actions and committee responsibilities
1.1. Promote free and open discovery of and access to geospatial data created and maintained by local governments and support efficient local to state data sharing.
1.2. Recommend utility infrastructure solutions that maintain data sharing security to aid discovery and ease of access to geospatial data.
1.3. Support priority initiatives that compile and maintain statewide geospatial datasets that benefit the businesses and citizens of North Carolina.
Access to local government datasetsLGC – promote value of data and share data hubs
Reduce redundant data requests and streamline processesSGUC – define technical and policy solutions to promote single source data collection and sharing through NC OneMap
Local data access constrained by security policyTAC - Propose secure data sharing options as recommended by providers.
Data standardization, development, and disseminationSMAC- Prioritize streams, and administrative boundaries, elevation, buildings footprints
Goal 1 Sub-goals 1.5 to 1.8 with associated actions and committee responsibilities
1.5. Request all state agencies to make the Council’s priority geospatial datasets discoverable and accessible through the NC OneMap Geospatial Portal.
1.6. Promote geospatial metadata for standard documentation.
1.7. Support enterprise applications that derive business value from geospatial data assets and analytics.
1.8. Promote efficient data sharing of large datasets by recommending technical solutions
Priority data accessible via NC OneMapSMAC – define priorities and make recommendations SGUC – define technical and policy solutions
Geospatial data documentation and protectionSMAC – provide metadata training resources and support implementation
Promote understanding of the enterprise capabilities of GISSGUC - educate management and IT departments about the value of GIS TAC – Assist with technical solutions
Promote access to previously difficult to share datasetsTAC- research solutions and make recommendations

Goal 2

Goal 2. Collaborate and conduct outreach for more integration of geospatial data in information technology for expanded benefits in the geospatial community in North Carolina.

Goal 2 Sub-goals 2.1 to 2.4 with associated actions and committee responsibilities
2.1. Identify opportunities to collaborate on GIS solutions in all state departments including divisions not directly represented on the Council to add value to state business processes.
2.2. Identify opportunities to collaborate on geospatial data and technical solutions on a regional basis, engaging councils of government, educational institutions, metropolitan planning organizations, and rural planning organizations.
2.3. Reach out to jurisdictions with the least resources to find ways to add value with geospatial data and applications.
2.4. Increase awareness and adoption of Council initiatives and priorities through outreach and education
Collaborate with State agencies to add value and prevent silos.SGUC – Identify needs and opportunities; optimize GIS and IT resources.
Collaborate on a regional basis, engaging councils of government.LGC – identify opportunities in working groups
Reach out to local governments with the least resourcesLGC – identify needs and practical ways to assist SMAC – assist in technical support
Engage the GIS community at the local, state, and federal level to promote initiativesLGC, SGUC, FIC – Share opportunities for collaboration, promote initiatives, promote the value of GIS
